_comment: Copy this file and edit it to build your own drift profile. JSON has no real comment syntax, so the docs live in _comment* keys, which the engine ignores. Every field below is optional: omit any and the engine substitutes a safe default (the values shown are the defaults). Numeric fields are clamped to the ranges noted.
_comment_name: Short label for this profile (max 64 chars). Shows up in scores and the status line.
name: custom
_comment_kind: 'lexical' uses this stdlib engine. 'judge' is reserved for an LLM-based scorer handled elsewhere; leave as 'lexical' unless you know you want that.
_comment_description: Free-text note about what this profile enforces. Documentation only; not used in scoring.
description: Describe what behavioral contract this profile enforces.
_comment_threshold: 0..100. A turn is flagged 'drift' when its score is >= threshold, else 'ok'. Lower = stricter.
_comment_sensitivity: 0.1..3.0. Linear multiplier on the final score before clamping. 1.0 = neutral, >1 = twitchier, <1 = calmer.
_comment_weights: Relative importance of each scoring component. Weights are relative (the max weight defines the scale), not a budget. Set a weight to 0 to disable that component. The lexical classes (hedges/filler/hype/meta) measure marker density; verbosity = words-per-sentence pressure; length = absolute prose length; complexity = long-word ratio.
_comment_lexicons: Optional. Override marker word/phrase lists per class. If you provide a class here it FULLY REPLACES the engine default for that class (it is not merged). Multi-word entries are matched as phrases; single words are matched as tokens. Omit this whole object to use the built-in defaults.
_comment_target_wps: 1..100. Sentences at/under this words-per-sentence contribute no verbosity pressure.
_comment_max_wps: 2..200. Words-per-sentence where the verbosity component saturates at full.
_comment_target_words: 1..5000. Prose word count under this is free (no length pressure).
_comment_max_words: 2..100000. Prose word count where the length component saturates.
_comment_long_word_len: 4..40. A token this many characters or longer counts as a 'long word' for the complexity component.
_comment_max_long_ratio: 0.01..1.0. Fraction of long words at which the complexity component saturates.
